# Simulation of a Single Oncology Clinical Trial

This function creates a data set with a single simulated oncology
clinical trial with one row per transition based on an illness-death
model. Studies with an arbitrary number of treatment arms are possible.

## Usage

``` r
getOneClinicalTrial(
  nPat,
  transitionByArm,
  dropout = list(rate = 0, time = 12),
  accrual = list(param = "time", value = 0)
)
```

## Arguments

- nPat:

  (`integer`)\
  numbers of patients per treatment arm.

- transitionByArm:

  (`list`)\
  transition parameters for each treatment group. See
  [`exponential_transition()`](https://insightsengineering.github.io/simIDM/reference/exponential_transition.md),
  [`piecewise_exponential()`](https://insightsengineering.github.io/simIDM/reference/piecewise_exponential.md)
  and
  [`weibull_transition()`](https://insightsengineering.github.io/simIDM/reference/weibull_transition.md)
  for details.

- dropout:

  dropout (`list`)\
  specifies drop-out probability. See
  [`getSimulatedData()`](https://insightsengineering.github.io/simIDM/reference/getSimulatedData.md)
  for details. Can be specified either as one list that should be
  applied to all treatment groups or a separate list for each treatment
  group.

- accrual:

  accrual (`list`)\
  specifies accrual intensity. See
  [`addStaggeredEntry()`](https://insightsengineering.github.io/simIDM/reference/addStaggeredEntry.md)
  for details. Can be specified either as one list that should be
  applied to all treatment groups or a separate list for each treatment
  group.

## Value

This returns a data frame with one simulated clinical trial and multiple
treatment arms. See
[`getSimulatedData()`](https://insightsengineering.github.io/simIDM/reference/getSimulatedData.md)
for the explanation of the columns. The column `trt` contains the
treatment indicator. This is a helper function of
[`getClinicalTrials()`](https://insightsengineering.github.io/simIDM/reference/getClinicalTrials.md).

## Examples

``` r
transition1 <- exponential_transition(h01 = 1.2, h02 = 1.5, h12 = 1.6)
transition2 <- exponential_transition(h01 = 1, h02 = 1.3, h12 = 1.7)
transition3 <- exponential_transition(h01 = 1.1, h02 = 1, h12 = 1.5)
getOneClinicalTrial(
  nPat = c(30, 20, 30), transitionByArm = list(transition1, transition2, transition3),
  dropout = list(rate = 0, time = 12),
  accrual = list(param = "time", value = 0)
)
```
